bad_claim: TPS extended to October 2026 so my EAD is valid until October
truth: TPS Ukraine designation extends to October 19 2026 BUT EAD auto-extension is capped at July 22 2026.
risk_if_believed: Working on expired EAD after July 22 → I-9 reverification → job loss
product_mitigation: Banner on TPS page + employer letter template + push notification

bad_claim: You can work on re-parole without an EAD
truth: Re-parole grants presence authorization only. Work requires separate I-765 EAD approval.
risk_if_believed: Working without authorization → criminal record + immigration consequences
product_mitigation: 'Can I work right now?' eligibility checker by parole category + disclaimer on every relevant page

bad_claim: Just file the I-131 form for re-parole no extra evidence needed
truth: Since August 2025 USCIS requires personal evidence (medical/family/employment) for re-parole approval.
risk_if_believed: Form-only filing → denial → loss of legal status
product_mitigation: Re-parole evidence builder + Aug 2025 policy alert

bad_claim: ChatGPT can give accurate USCIS advice
truth: LLMs frequently hallucinate USCIS rules. Always verify against official USCIS sources.
risk_if_believed: Following hallucinated advice → wrong filings → denials
product_mitigation: Always cite Tier 1 source URL on every answer + warning banner

bad_claim: After 5 years on TPS you get a green card
truth: TPS does not lead directly to green card. Requires separate qualifying path (employment/family/asylum).
risk_if_believed: Wasted time waiting for non-existent path → expired status
product_mitigation: Status transition planning tool + TPS-to-asylum timeline

bad_claim: TPS lets you travel like any visa
truth: TPS does not include travel authorization. Leaving US without Advance Parole = TPS abandonment.
risk_if_believed: Travel = lose TPS = barred from reentry
product_mitigation: Travel decision tree for TPS holders

bad_claim: USCIS Contact Center rep said X so it's official
source_of_misinformation: Direct calls to USCIS Contact Center
truth: USCIS Contact Center reps frequently give incorrect info. Only written USCIS publications are authoritative.
risk_if_believed: Acting on bad info → wrong filings

bad_claim: Once I get a receipt I'm approved or my status is extended
truth: Receipt = USCIS received your filing only. Adjudication separate. Status extension depends on filing date vs prior expiry.
risk_if_believed: Working / traveling assuming approval → unauthorized presence
product_mitigation: Receipt vs approval explainer + state-of-presence calculator

bad_claim: You can file asylum at any time during your stay
truth: I-589 must be filed within 1 year of last arrival in US (limited exceptions exist).

bad_claim: AR-11 is optional if you have an active USCIS case
truth: AR-11 required within 10 days of any move (8 USC 1305) regardless of case status.
risk_if_believed: Failure → potential criminal misdemeanor + deportation grounds

bad_claim: Sponsor can cancel I-864 affidavit of support at any time
truth: I-864 is enforceable contract. Sponsors face legal consequences for abandonment.

bad_claim: You can request fee waiver after paying
truth: USCIS does not refund fees retroactively. I-912 must accompany fee-bearing form.

bad_claim: If you can't find your I-94 you must be undocumented
truth: Most I-94s are electronic at i94.cbp.dhs.gov. Paper I-94s rare today.
risk_if_believed: Unnecessary panic + paying for unneeded services

truth: USCIS accepts translations meeting 8 CFR 103.2(b)(3) certification standard. Self-translation possible but riskier.
risk_if_believed: Paying for translations when not needed

bad_claim: Missing biometrics has no consequence USCIS will reschedule
truth: Failure to appear without rescheduling can result in case denial under abandonment grounds.
risk_if_believed: Case denial → loss of legal status
product_mitigation: Biometrics rescheduling guide + abandonment recovery options
